description: This figure presents a comprehensive stacked bar chart illustrating the quantitative decomposition of calculated yield strength (YS) for a duplex lightweight steel (Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni) based on multiple strengthening mechanisms. The y-axis indicates strength contribution in MPa, while individual bars represent distinct contributions: hetero-deformation induced (HDI) strengthening (597 MPa, red), lattice friction stress of the austenite phase weighted by volume fraction (σ₀,γf_γ = 78 MPa, orange), solid solution strengthening in austenite (σ_ss,γf_γ = 182 MPa, yellow), dislocation strengthening in austenite (σ_dis,γf_γ = 85 MPa, green), VC carbide precipitation strengthening (σ_VC f_γ = 74 MPa, light blue), L1₂ ordered domain strengthening (σ_LRO f_γ = 119 MPa, dark blue), lattice friction stress of the B2 phase (σ₀,B₂f_B₂ = 135 MPa, purple), and dislocation strengthening in B2 phase (σ_dis,B₂f_B₂ = 46 MPa, dark purple). The rightmost composite bar displays the experimental yield strength (Exp. σ_YS ≈ 1316 MPa) as the cumulative sum of all contributions. The visualization reveals that HDI strengthening constitutes the dominant contribution (~45% of total YS), followed by solid solution strengthening in austenite and lattice friction of the B2 phase, quantitatively validating the ultrahigh strength achieved through dual-nanoprecipitation design.
